article_meta.title: Politics Trump signs dozens of executive or

reasoning: The model correctly identifies three genuine bias instances in the author's own prose. No quoted text is flagged. The bias type labels are reasonable and defensible. There are a few minor potential misses but overall this is a strong analysis.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged items are clearly from the author's own prose, not from quoted or attributed source language. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three flagged items are defensible. 'Wasted no time' is indeed interpretive spin. 'Systematic dismantling' uses loaded language. 'Pet projects' is clearly a subjective diminishing characterization. Perhaps 'wasted no time' is borderline as it's a common journalistic idiom, but it's still defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Some additional bias instances could have been flagged: 'avalanche of Day 1 actions' (used twice - sensational/loaded word choice), 'flurry of activity' (similar), 'violent, fentanyl-peddling Mexican drug cartels' (the author's own characterization using loaded adjectives - though this is arguably factual), 'fulfilling a key conservative wishlist item' (interpretive framing), 'made good on his campaign pledge' (positive spin implying promise-keeping). Missing 'avalanche' and 'wishlist item' are notable omissions.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The labels are well-applied. 'Spin' for 'wasted no time' and 'systematic dismantling' are correct - these are interpretive verb/framing choices. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for 'pet projects' is appropriate as it's a diminishing descriptor. All labels fit the defined categories.
